title: Parameter Panel UI (Sliders, Numbers, Toggles for Exposed Node Parameters)
description: Create a parameter panel that displays the exposed parameters of the currently selected node group and allows the user to modify them with appropriate widgets. Float parameters get sliders with min/max range. Integer parameters get stepper controls. Boolean parameters get toggle switches. Vector3 parameters get three linked number inputs (X/Y/Z). When a parameter changes, the node graph is re-evaluated and the viewport updates live. This panel sits on the right side of the MeshNodeViewerScene. This is the 'Spore editor' parameter tweaking interface.
definition-of-done: 1. ParameterPanel class exists in ixdar-app/src/main/java/ixdar/gui/ui/. 2. Renders a vertical list of parameter widgets based on a node group's exposed parameters. 3. FloatSlider widget: draggable slider with min/max/current value display. 4. IntStepper widget: increment/decrement buttons with current value. 5. BoolToggle widget: clickable toggle with on/off visual state. 6. Vec3Input widget: three FloatSliders for X/Y/Z. 7. Changing any widget triggers a callback that re-evaluates the node graph. 8. Panel scrolls if parameters exceed visible area.
testing-plan: 1. Create a node group with float, int, boolean, and vector3 parameters. 2. Display in ParameterPanel, verify all widget types render. 3. Drag a float slider, verify the value updates and the mesh re-renders. 4. Click a boolean toggle, verify it flips and triggers re-evaluation. 5. Step an integer, verify the value changes by 1. 6. Verify scroll works with 10+ parameters.
todos: Create ParameterPanel container class with layout logic
Implement FloatSlider widget with drag interaction and value display
Implement BoolToggle widget with click interaction
Implement Vec3Input widget composing three FloatSliders
Wire parameter change callbacks to node graph re-evaluation
Add scroll support for the parameter list
Integrate into MeshNodeViewerScene right-side panel area
unknowns: Whether to support undo/redo for parameter changes
Whether to display parameter names from the node definition or allow custom display names
